member.name: Lilian Greenwood
member.synopsis: Lilian Greenwood is the Labour MP for Nottingham South , and has been an MP continually since 6 May 2010. She currently holds the Government posts of Parliamentary Under-Secretary (Department for Transport), and Government Whip, Lord Commissioner of HM Treasury.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Domestic Abuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many domestic violence easements were granted in (a) 2023 and (b) 2024 to date.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not readily available and to provide it would incur disproportionate cost.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness of the Care Quality Commission in enforcement of the Accessible Information Standard across the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Compliance with the Accessible Information Standard (AIS) is not directly assured by the Care Quality Commission (CQC). However, the performance of organisations in meeting people’s needs is considered in CQC assessment and ratings. Where the CQC has info...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Youth Services: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to provide adequate funding for the youth sector (a) workforce and (b) infrastructure needed to deliver the National Youth Guarantee.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the vital role that youth services and activities, and the workforce that delivers them, play in improving the life chances and wellbeing of young people.While local authorities are responsible for delivering youth provision, the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Nottingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the bi-mode trains on air quality in (a) Nottingham Railway station and (b) the surrounding area.
writtenQuestions.answerText: East Midlands Railway is replacing its diesel-only Class 222 trains with bi-mode Class 810s as part of the East Midlands franchise award. These new bi-mode trains will gradually be introduced into service starting in early 2025 and are anticipated to sign...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Midland Main Line: Electrification
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what progress his Department has made on the (a) planning and (b) programming for the electrification of the Midland Main Line to Nottingham.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Work is already underway to electrify the Midland Main Line (MML) with progress being made to install overhead line equipment between Kettering and Wigston, as well as upgrades south of Bedford to be completed in 2025. During 2024 Network Rail are continu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport
